message:
chore(beads): Update issue tracker with async error coverage feature

Beads automatic sync after recent commits. New issue created for tracking
async error path coverage implementation across all 7 languages.

Changes:
- Added ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j: "Implement async error path coverage"
  - Type: feature
  - Priority: 1 (high)
  - Status: in_progress
  - Assignee: codex
  - Scope: JS/Python/Go/C++/Rust/Java/Ruby async error handling detection

Updated metadata:
- beads.base.meta.json: Updated commit reference to 5bd168e
- beads.left.meta.json: Updated commit reference to 2aa61a1
- Timestamps refreshed to track issue tracker state

Issue details:
The e3j issue tracks the full implementation of async error path coverage,
including:
- ast-grep rule implementation for all languages
- Buggy and clean fixtures demonstrating patterns
- Manifest test case integration
- UBS validation runs

Current progress (tracked in async-error-coverage-todo.md):
- ✅ JavaScript: Complete (3 rules)
- ✅ Python: Complete (2 rules)
- ✅ C++: Complete (3 rules)
- ✅ Go: Complete (3 rules)
- ✅ Java: Complete (2 rules)
- ✅ Rust: Complete (2 rules)
- ⏳ Ruby: Metadata only (1 rule pending implementation)
- ☐ Fixtures and tests: Pending for all languages

This issue will be closed once all scanner implementations are complete,
fixtures are added, manifest tests pass, and UBS runs validate the rules
fire correctly.
